title: Free Online Tools for UPSC, SSC, and Banking Exam Preparation India 2026
summary: Preparing for UPSC, SSC, or banking exams? These free tools help with age eligibility, date calculations, answer writing practice, and study planning.
content:
# Free Tools for UPSC, SSC and Banking Exam Preparation

## Essential Calculators for Government Exam Aspirants

### Age Calculator — Check Eligibility Instantly
Age limits for major exams:
- UPSC CSE: 21–32 (General), 21–35 (OBC), 21–37 (SC/ST)
- SSC CGL: 18–32 (General)
- IBPS PO: 20–30 (General)
- SBI PO: 21–30 (General)

Use the [Age Calculator](/tools/age-calculator.html) for exact age in years, months, and days as of any cut-off date.

### Date Calculator — Plan Your Preparation
How many days until the exam? How many weeks of study left? Our [Date Calculator](/tools/date-calculator.html) answers instantly.

### Word Counter — For UPSC Mains Answer Writing
UPSC Mains has strict limits: 150 words for 10-mark questions, 250 words for 15-mark questions. The [Word Counter](/tools/word-counter.html) is essential for answer practice.

### Percentage Calculator — Convert Scores
Convert raw marks to percentage, calculate cutoffs, find how much more you need. Use the [Percentage Calculator](/tools/percentage-calculator.html).

## Study Planning with AI — How Toppers Use AI Tools

**Generate study schedules:**
"Create a 6-month UPSC Prelims study plan. I have 5 hours/day. Include subject rotation and mock test schedule."

**Create question banks:**
"Generate 20 MCQ questions on Indian Polity — Article 356, emergency provisions. Include answers and explanations."

**Essay outlines:**
"Create an outline for a 1,000-word UPSC essay on 'Technology as an enabler of inclusive growth in India'."

## Important Limitations
- Cannot replace NCERT — use AI to supplement, not replace
- May have outdated current affairs — always check The Hindu, PIB
- AI-generated MCQs need cross-verification against official sources
